_doc: Integration Service dispatch root. After the shared trunk gathers the failing job + exception/logs and extracts the signature, this node routes to the per-playbook subtree. evaluator:exact for code/FQN tokens; judgment for the message-match entries (the LLM-walk confirms those). Each goto is a generated subtree under generated/. Built from references/exception-table.md (IS rows) + the per-playbook trees.

nodes._reads_from: the shared trunk's job-get/logs gather: error_code (regex DAP-(GE|RT)-\d+), exception_fqn, and the verbatim message

children.judgment_q: Is the NRE on/after a connector activity (stack shows ForEach over a SWEntities.*_List connector output)? If yes route to the IS NRE tree; if the NRE is in another package (Excel/UIA), dispatch to that domain instead.

when.value: connection is invalid or you do not have access

children.judgment_q: Does the verbatim error name the IS 'connection invalid or no access' condition (vs a disabled connection, which is DAP-GE-3005 -> general-exception)?

nodes.reason: IS signature not covered by a built subtree (e.g. connection-auth-expired, operation-failed, trigger-not-firing — no tree yet). Hand to the LLM hypothesis loop seeded with the trace + the matched IS playbook from references/exception-table.md.

_coverage: 6 of 9 IS playbooks have generated subtrees (the 3 uncovered have no dedicated test fixture to ground a build; they fall to the LLM loop via the default).
